seoTitle: How to Make a Mobile-Friendly Website in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to build a mobile-friendly website in Canva, keeping layouts responsive and easy to navigate on smaller screens.
intro: Since most visitors browse on their phones, a website needs to look and function well on smaller screens, and Canva's website builder includes tools to help with that. This guide covers designing and checking a site for mobile friendliness.

steps.heading: Start a new Canva website project
steps.body: Choose a website template or start from a blank design in Canva's website builder.

steps.heading: Use responsive layout elements
steps.body: Favor Canva's built-in website blocks and sections, which are designed to adapt automatically across screen sizes.

steps.heading: Preview the mobile version
steps.body: Use Canva's device preview toggle to see how your site looks specifically on a phone screen.

steps.heading: Adjust spacing and text size
steps.body: Fix any sections where text feels cramped or images are cut off on the mobile preview.

steps.heading: Publish and test on a real device
steps.body: Once published, open the live site on your own phone to confirm everything looks and works as expected.

faq.question: Does Canva automatically make websites mobile-friendly?
faq.answer: Canva's website builder is designed with responsive layouts, but it's still worth checking the mobile preview and adjusting as needed.

faq.question: Can I set different layouts for mobile and desktop?
faq.answer: Canva primarily auto-adapts a single design, though you can fine-tune spacing and stacking for smaller screens.

faq.question: Is Canva's website builder free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, a version is available for free, with additional features and a custom domain available on paid plans.

faq.question: How do I check my site works well on mobile before publishing?
faq.answer: Use the built-in preview mode to switch between desktop and mobile views before you publish.
